16	
17	// Env-variable helpers for Helm-driven configuration (Phase 3 plan 03-09).
18	//
19	// These helpers exist as testable units (cmd/manager/env_test.go) because
20	// the main() function itself is not unit-testable (it constructs the
21	// controller-runtime Manager and blocks on mgr.Start). Helpers cover the
22	// per-level model defaults (D-C4) and leader-election tuning (D-D1).
23	package main
24	
25	import (
26		"os"
27		"strconv"
28		"time"
29	
30		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/controller"
31	)
32	
33	// envOrDefault returns the value of the env var named key, or fallback when
34	// the env var is unset or empty. Empty-string is treated as unset so a Helm
35	// value left at its zero default (e.g. `value: ""`) cleanly falls through to
36	// the binary's compile-time default rather than overriding it with "".
37	func envOrDefault(key, fallback string) string {
38		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
39			return v
40		}
41		return fallback
42	}
43	
44	// atoiOrDefault returns the integer value of the env var named key, or
45	// fallback when the env var is unset, empty, or not an integer.
46	// Non-integer values are tolerated (return fallback) so a stray non-numeric
47	// value in the Helm chart cannot crash-loop the controller.
48	func atoiOrDefault(key string, fallback int) int {
49		v := os.Getenv(key)
50		if v == "" {
51			return fallback
52		}
53		n, err := strconv.Atoi(v)
54		if err != nil {
55			return fallback
56		}
57		return n
58	}
59	
60	// resolvePerLevelModels returns the four-key map from level name
61	// ("milestone"|"phase"|"plan"|"task") to the model identifier used as the
62	// last-fallback in dispatch_helpers.ResolveProvider (D-C2).
63	//
64	// Defaults match D-C4 — phase brief lock-in:
65	//
66	//	milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
67	//	phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
68	//	plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
69	//	task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
70	//
71	// Override per-level via env vars (set by the manager Deployment from Helm
72	// values.yaml subagent.levels.{level}.model):
73	//
74	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE
75	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE
76	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN
77	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK
78	func resolvePerLevelModels() map[string]string {
79		return map[string]string{
80			"milestone": envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE", "claude-opus-4-7"),
81			"phase":     envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
82			"plan":      envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
83			"task":      envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK", "claude-haiku-4-5"),
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// tideHelmProviderDefaults builds the controller.ProviderDefaults struct
88	// (D-C2 last-fallback in ResolveProvider) from the Helm-chart subagent image
89	// + per-level model map. claudeImage is the default subagent image
90	// (CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var); per-level models come from
91	// resolvePerLevelModels (TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_* env vars).
92	//
93	// Called once at Manager startup; the result is stamped onto each up-stack
94	// reconciler's HelmProviderDefaults field (MilestoneReconciler /
95	// PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler).
96	func tideHelmProviderDefaults(claudeImage string) controller.ProviderDefaults {
97		return controller.ProviderDefaults{
98			Image:  claudeImage,
99			Models: resolvePerLevelModels(),
100		}
101	}
102	
103	// resolveLeaderElectionTiming returns the (lease, renew, retry) durations
104	// for controller-runtime's LeaderElection block (D-D1 / Phase 3 chaos-resume).
105	//
106	// Defaults pin to common controller-runtime production values
107	// (lease=15s, renew=10s, retry=2s) which give a single-pod restart ≤25s
108	// failover ceiling, with the invariant lease > renew > retry preserved.
109	// Override via env vars (set by manager Deployment from Helm values.yaml
110	// leaderElection.*):
111	//
112	//	TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS  (default 15)
113	//	TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS  (default 10)
114	//	TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS  (default 2)
115	func resolveLeaderElectionTiming() (lease, renew, retry time.Duration) {
116		lease = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS", 15)) * time.Second
117		renew = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS", 10)) * time.Second
118		retry = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS", 2)) * time.Second
119		return lease, renew, retry
120	}
121

1	# examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml
2	#
3	# Run 2 of 3 in the TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood sequence.
4	# Apply ONLY after 01-analytics-project reaches `status.phase: Complete`.
5	# Do NOT apply 03-project-editor-project.yaml until this Project reaches
6	# `status.phase: Complete` — the Project CRD has no ordering mechanism;
7	# sequencing is operator discipline.
8	#
9	# Purpose: add a second Subagent implementation (Codex / OpenAI) to TIDE and
10	# demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — actually running mixed-provider
11	# waves, not just proving the interface compiles.
12	#
13	# After this run completes: the run's token spend, dispatch behavior, and
14	# failure characteristics are visible on the analytics dashboard built by run 1.
15	# Run 3 (Project editor) can then be authored and applied through the dashboard
16	# surface run 3 itself will build.
17	#
18	# Prerequisites (see README.md for full recipe):
19	#   - 01-analytics-project must have reached status.phase: Complete
20	#   - kind cluster + TIDE charts installed (see docs/INSTALL.md)
21	#   - tide-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
22	#     (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + GIT_PAT — same as analytics namespace)
23	#   - openai-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
24	#     (OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY — see README.md)
25	#   - per-namespace-resources applied per README.md
26	---
27	apiVersion: v1
28	kind: Namespace
29	metadata:
30	  name: tide-dogfood-codex
31	  labels:
32	    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
33	    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
34	    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
35	---
36	apiVersion: tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1
37	kind: Project
38	metadata:
39	  name: dogfood-codex-runtime
40	  namespace: tide-dogfood-codex
41	  labels:
42	    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
43	    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
44	    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
45	spec:
46	  # TIDE operates against its own repo — TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood run.
47	  targetRepo: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git
48	
49	  outcomePrompt: |
50	    ## Outcome
51	
52	    Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex runtime (OpenAI) and
53	    demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually
54	    run, not just interface scaffolding. Concrete outcome: a TIDE operator can
55	    configure a Project where the planner pool runs Claude and the executor pool
56	    runs Codex, and TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct
57	    wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.
58	
59	    ## Architecture — locked decisions (do NOT reinvent these)
60	
61	    **Implementation location: internal/subagent/codex/**
62	    Mirror the existing internal/subagent/anthropic/ layering exactly:
63	    - internal/subagent/codex/client.go — Client struct + constructor
64	    - internal/subagent/codex/run.go — Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
65	    - internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile — multi-stage build mirroring anthropic/Dockerfile
66	    - internal/subagent/codex/doc.go — package doc referencing D-C1 layering pattern
67	
68	    All OpenAI/Codex-specific code stays behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent
69	    interface. No provider hard-coding in the controller. No Codex imports
70	    in internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.
71	
72	    **Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project).**
73	    Planner pool and executor pool each pin a runtime. This is the correct
74	    granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves real
75	    heterogeneity within a single run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity
76	    of per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen):
77	    - Per-task selection: large API surface, non-trivial scheduler changes.
78	    - Per-project selection: no mixing within a run (defeats heterogeneity goal).
79	
80	    The SubagentConfig field already has Levels.{Milestone,Phase,Plan,Task}
81	    per-level LevelConfig overrides (api/v1alpha1/project_types.go). Wire the
82	    Codex image at the levels the operator wants to run Codex.
83	
84	    **Credential path.**
85	    OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY via a K8s Secret — the established
86	    Secret-ref pattern from providerSecretRef. Never mount host config, never
87	    headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies
88	    to Codex). Chart values for the new Secret name are part of the deliverable.
89	
90	    **Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics.**
91	    The spec's failure handling (spec section "Failure handling at wave
92	    boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
93	    - Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
94	      independent).
95	    - Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
96	    - Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
97	    This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers.
98	
99	    **Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
100	    - codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
101	      to stdout.
102	    - Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
103	      --ignore-user-config.
104	    - --json emits a JSONL event stream.
105	    - --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
106	      — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
107	    - Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
108	      (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
109	    - API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
110	      no headless-OAuth problem.
111	    - MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or
112	      codex mcp add — available if needed for child-CRD emission.
113	    - Sources: developers.openai.com/codex/{noninteractive,cli/reference,
114	      auth,cli/features} (verified 2026-06).
115	
116	    **Container image.**
117	    internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile must build clean. The final image
118	    bundles the Codex CLI binary. Image reference convention mirrors
119	    ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0 (replace "claude" with
120	    "codex" in the image name; exact tag/registry are authoring decisions).
121	
122	    ## Constraints
123	
124	    - All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in internal/subagent/codex/ and
125	      behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent interface — no provider leakage into
126	      the controller or cmd/manager.
127	    - Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves.
128	    - Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — reference
129	      by Secret name only.
130	    - Follow all CLAUDE.md conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, logr/zap logging,
131	      controller-runtime patterns.
132	    - Do NOT break existing tests. Confirm make test is green before
133	      authoring any plan as delivered.
134	    - Do NOT add a real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit test-env
135	      guard (OPENAI_API_KEY env set) — tests must pass offline.
136	
137	  # Phase 2 D-C1..C4: credproxy reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from this Secret.
138	  # For the planner pool (Claude side of the heterogeneous run).
139	  # The Codex executor pool's OPENAI_API_KEY lives in a separate Secret —
140	  # see README.md for the openai-secrets creation step.
141	  providerSecretRef: tide-secrets
142	
143	  # Starting cap for dogfood runs — tune before applying.
144	  # These are multi-phase real work runs; the cap is a safety net.
145	  budget:
146	    absoluteCapCents: 10000
147	    rollingWindowCapCents: 10000
148	    rollingWindowDuration: 24h
149	
150	  # Default image + model for the planner pool (Claude).
151	  # Codex executor pool is wired via subagent.levels.task.image override
152	  # once the codex subagent image is built by this run.
153	  # Cheaper alternatives: model: claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5
154	  subagent:
155	    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0
156	    model: claude-opus-4-7
157	
158	  # Phase 3 D-B6: per-project git target + creds.
159	  git:
160	    repoURL: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git
161	    credsSecretRef: tide-secrets
162	
163	  # Human oversight at milestone and phase level; plan and task auto.
164	  # Fully-autonomous: change milestone and phase to auto.
165	  gates:
166	    milestone: approve
167	    phase: approve
168	    plan: auto
169	    task: auto
170	    pauseBetweenWaves: false
171	
172	  planAdmission:
173	    fileTouchMode: strict
174	
175	  maxAttemptsPerTask: 3
176

# examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml
#
# Run 2 of 3 in the TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood sequence.
# Apply ONLY after 01-analytics-project reaches `status.phase: Complete`.
# Do NOT apply 03-project-editor-project.yaml until this Project reaches
# `status.phase: Complete` — the Project CRD has no ordering mechanism;
# sequencing is operator discipline.
#
# Purpose: add a second Subagent implementation (Codex / OpenAI) to TIDE and
# demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — actually running mixed-provider
# waves, not just proving the interface compiles.
#
# After this run completes: the run's token spend, dispatch behavior, and
# failure characteristics are visible on the analytics dashboard built by run 1.
# Run 3 (Project editor) can then be authored and applied through the dashboard
# surface run 3 itself will build.
#
# Prerequisites (see README.md for full recipe):
#   - 01-analytics-project must have reached status.phase: Complete
#   - kind cluster + TIDE charts installed (see docs/INSTALL.md)
#   - tide-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
#     (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + GIT_PAT — same as analytics namespace)
#   - openai-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
#     (OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY — see README.md)
#   - per-namespace-resources applied per README.md
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: tide-dogfood-codex
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
---
apiVersion: tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1
kind: Project
metadata:
  name: dogfood-codex-runtime
  namespace: tide-dogfood-codex
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
spec:
  # TIDE operates against its own repo — TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood run.
  targetRepo: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git

  outcomePrompt: |
    ## Outcome

    Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex runtime (OpenAI) and
    demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually
    run, not just interface scaffolding. Concrete outcome: a TIDE operator can
    configure a Project where the planner pool runs Claude and the executor pool
    runs Codex, and TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct
    wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.

    ## Architecture — locked decisions (do NOT reinvent these)

    **Implementation location: internal/subagent/codex/**
    Mirror the existing internal/subagent/anthropic/ layering exactly:
    - internal/subagent/codex/client.go — Client struct + constructor
    - internal/subagent/codex/run.go — Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
    - internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile — multi-stage build mirroring anthropic/Dockerfile
    - internal/subagent/codex/doc.go — package doc referencing D-C1 layering pattern

    All OpenAI/Codex-specific code stays behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent
    interface. No provider hard-coding in the controller. No Codex imports
    in internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.

    **Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project).**
    Planner pool and executor pool each pin a runtime. This is the correct
    granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves real
    heterogeneity within a single run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity
    of per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen):
    - Per-task selection: large API surface, non-trivial scheduler changes.
    - Per-project selection: no mixing within a run (defeats heterogeneity goal).

    The SubagentConfig field already has Levels.{Milestone,Phase,Plan,Task}
    per-level LevelConfig overrides (api/v1alpha1/project_types.go). Wire the
    Codex image at the levels the operator wants to run Codex.

    **Credential path.**
    OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY via a K8s Secret — the established
    Secret-ref pattern from providerSecretRef. Never mount host config, never
    headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies
    to Codex). Chart values for the new Secret name are part of the deliverable.

    **Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics.**
    The spec's failure handling (spec section "Failure handling at wave
    boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
    - Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
      independent).
    - Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
    - Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
    This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers.

    **Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
    - codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
      to stdout.
    - Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
      --ignore-user-config.
    - --json emits a JSONL event stream.
    - --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
      — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
    - Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
      (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
    - API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
      no headless-OAuth problem.
    - MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or
      codex mcp add — available if needed for child-CRD emission.
    - Sources: developers.openai.com/codex/{noninteractive,cli/reference,
      auth,cli/features} (verified 2026-06).

    **Container image.**
    internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile must build clean. The final image
    bundles the Codex CLI binary. Image reference convention mirrors
    ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0 (replace "claude" with
    "codex" in the image name; exact tag/registry are authoring decisions).

    ## Constraints

    - All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in internal/subagent/codex/ and
      behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent interface — no provider leakage into
      the controller or cmd/manager.
    - Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves.
    - Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — reference
      by Secret name only.
    - Follow all CLAUDE.md conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, logr/zap logging,
      controller-runtime patterns.
    - Do NOT break existing tests. Confirm make test is green before
      authoring any plan as delivered.
    - Do NOT add a real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit test-env
      guard (OPENAI_API_KEY env set) — tests must pass offline.

  # Phase 2 D-C1..C4: credproxy reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from this Secret.
  # For the planner pool (Claude side of the heterogeneous run).
  # The Codex executor pool's OPENAI_API_KEY lives in a separate Secret —
  # see README.md for the openai-secrets creation step.
  providerSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # Starting cap for dogfood runs — tune before applying.
  # These are multi-phase real work runs; the cap is a safety net.
  budget:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000
    rollingWindowCapCents: 10000
    rollingWindowDuration: 24h

  # Default image + model for the planner pool (Claude).
  # Codex executor pool is wired via subagent.levels.task.image override
  # once the codex subagent image is built by this run.
  # Cheaper alternatives: model: claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5
  subagent:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0
    model: claude-opus-4-7

  # Phase 3 D-B6: per-project git target + creds.
  git:
    repoURL: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git
    credsSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # Human oversight at milestone and phase level; plan and task auto.
  # Fully-autonomous: change milestone and phase to auto.
  gates:
    milestone: approve
    phase: approve
    plan: auto
    task: auto
    pauseBetweenWaves: false

  planAdmission:
    fileTouchMode: strict

  maxAttemptsPerTask: 3

# Phase Brief — phase-01-provider-switch

**Phase:** `phase-01-provider-switch`
**Milestone:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
**Wave position:** Milestone wave 1 — no upstream phase dependencies; runs in
parallel with any other milestone-wave-1 phase.

---

## Objective

Extend the TIDE controller's subagent resolution machinery with a
**per-level vendor field** — so a single Project CRD can declare that its
planner levels run Claude and its executor levels run Codex, and
`ResolveProvider` delivers the correct `ProviderSpec.Vendor` to each
dispatched Job without any Codex-specific code touching the controller or
`cmd/manager`.

Today `ResolveProvider` (`internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go`) returns
`Vendor:"anthropic"` unconditionally — a non-Anthropic image at a level is
*selected* by `resolveImage` but then *rejected* at runtime by the image's
vendor sentinel check (`vendorSentinel != "anthropic"` → fail-fast refuse).
This phase closes that gap. It is a pure controller-side refactor: no Codex
CLI, no container image, no usage/pricing code — those belong to
phase-02-codex-subagent-core and phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint.

---

## Scope

**In scope:**

- Add `Vendor string` to `LevelConfig` and `SubagentConfig` in
  `api/v1alpha1/project_types.go`; add CEL validation marker (`+kubebuilder:validation:Enum=anthropic;openai`)
  so the webhook rejects unknown vendors at admission time.
- Re-run `make manifests generate` to propagate the new field into
  `zz_generated.deepcopy.go` and the CRD YAML under `config/crd/bases/`.
- Add `Vendor string` to `ProviderDefaults`; rewire `ResolveProvider` to
  resolve vendor down the precedence chain:
  `Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → helmDefaults.Vendor → "anthropic"`.
- Thread the Helm-chart vendor default through `cmd/manager/env.go`
  (a `resolveDefaultVendor()` helper, `tideHelmProviderDefaults` extended) and
  the manager Deployment template (`TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR` env var).
- Add `subagent.defaults.vendor` (and optional per-level vendor keys) to
  `charts/tide/values.yaml`; wire them into `charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml`.

**Out of scope (later phases):**

- The Codex subagent package itself (`internal/subagent/codex/`).
- The Codex container image and entrypoint (`cmd/codex-subagent/`).
- Helm chart Secret wiring for `OPENAI_API_KEY` — that is
  phase-05-chart-secret-wiring.
- The heterogeneous integration example and end-to-end test — phase-06.

---

## Deliverables

| Deliverable | Verification |
|---|---|
| `LevelConfig.Vendor` + `SubagentConfig.Vendor` fields with CEL enum marker | `make manifests generate` exits 0; deepcopy regenerated |
| `ResolveProvider` vendor precedence chain | Unit test: Codex-pinned level → `"openai"`, Claude-pinned level → `"anthropic"`, default → `"anthropic"` |
| `ProviderDefaults.Vendor` + `resolveDefaultVendor()` in `cmd/manager/env.go` | `env_test.go` round-trip: `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR=openai` → `ProviderDefaults.Vendor=="openai"` |
| `subagent.defaults.vendor` Helm value + `deployment.yaml` `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR` env | `helm template` renders correct env value |
| `make verify-import-firewall` + `make verify-dispatch-imports` green | No `openai` or `codex` package appears in `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` |
| `make test` exits 0 | All existing + new unit tests pass |

---

## Verification Gates

1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit tests plus the new vendor-resolution
   suite pass offline with no network calls.
2. `make manifests generate` exits 0 — CRD YAML and deepcopy are regenerated
   from the updated types; no manual edits to generated files.
3. `make verify-import-firewall` and `make verify-dispatch-imports` are green —
   zero Codex/OpenAI imports visible in `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.
4. Unit test explicitly proves: in one Project, a `Levels.Task.Vendor="openai"`
   override resolves `Vendor:"openai"` while a `Levels.Phase` with no vendor
   override resolves `Vendor:"anthropic"`.
5. `helm template` renders the `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR` env var in the manager
   Deployment under both the default path and an explicit `--set` override.

---

## Plan Decomposition

Three Plans in a linear dependency chain — each plan has a clean,
non-overlapping file set enforced by the admission webhook.

```
plan-01-vendor-api-types
        ↓
plan-02-resolve-provider-vendor
        ↓
plan-03-manager-helm-vendor
```

**Wave assignment (illustrative — authoritative wave derived by Kahn over
the Task DAG):**

| Plan | Wave | Depends on |
|---|---|---|
| `plan-01-vendor-api-types` | 1 | — |
| `plan-02-resolve-provider-vendor` | 2 | `plan-01-vendor-api-types` |
| `plan-03-manager-helm-vendor` | 3 | `plan-02-resolve-provider-vendor` |

`charts/tide/values.yaml` and `charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml` to add `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR` + `subagent.defaults.vendor

`!

-04 already handled:

- The `subagent.defaults.vendor` configuration in values

So phase-05 should focus on two

handling per-level vendor overrides in values where individual levels can specify their own vendor defaults

input.command:
cat > /workspace/envelopes/80621bfb-519b-469c-ad37-49281e0b0dad/children/PHASE.md << 'PHASEBRIEF'
# Phase Brief — phase-05-chart-secret-wiring

**Phase:** `phase-05-chart-secret-wiring`
**Milestone:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
**Wave position:** Milestone wave 2 — depends on `phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch`; runs in
parallel with no other currently-planned milestone phases.

---

## Objective

Wire the Codex image default, the Codex provider-Secret reference, and the
`OPENAI_API_KEY` env-injection into dispatch Job pods — closing the gap between
phase-04's resolved `Vendor:"openai"` output and an actual runnable Codex Job
pod. Phase-04 delivers the per-level vendor switch in the controller; this
phase delivers the Helm chart surface that operators configure and the
controller-side secret injection that makes the vendor selection executable.

Three orthogonal concerns — Helm defaults, the Project CRD API field, and
the Job-pod secret injection — compose into a complete operator experience:
set `subagent.levels.task.vendor: openai` (or
`project.spec.subagent.levels.task.vendor: openai`) plus
`project.spec.codexProviderSecretRef: my-openai-secret`, and TIDE dispatches
Codex Jobs with `OPENAI_API_KEY` populated — no credproxy required, no host
config mounted, no OAuth.

---

## Scope

**In scope:**

- Add `images.codexSubagent` image block to `charts/tide/values.yaml` —
  mirrors the existing `claudeSubagent` entry; provides the cluster-wide
  Codex image default (`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`).
- Add `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` env var to `charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml`
  — the second image-default channel alongside `CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE`,
  consumed by the controller's `tideHelmProviderDefaults`.
- Extend `ProviderDefaults` with a `CodexImage string` field
  (`internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go`); make `resolveImage`
  vendor-aware — when the resolved vendor is `"openai"` the fallback is
  `helmDefaults.CodexImage` instead of `helmDefaults.Image`.
- Populate `ProviderDefaults.CodexImage` from `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` in
  `cmd/manager/env.go` (the `tideHelmProviderDefaults` constructor).
- Add `CodexProviderSecretRef string` to `ProjectSpec`
  (`api/v1alpha1/project_types.go`) — the per-Project Secret name carrying
  `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `CODEX_API_KEY`; mirrors the existing
  `providerSecretRef` field. Regenerate `zz_generated.deepcopy.go`.
- Extend all four Job-creating controller reconcilers
  (`task_controller.go`, `milestone_controller.go`, `phase_controller.go`,
  `plan_controller.go`) to inject `OPENAI_API_KEY` from
  `project.Spec.CodexProviderSecretRef` when `ResolveProvider` returns
  `Vendor:"openai"` for the dispatched level — following the same
  `envFrom: [{secretRef: {name: …}}]` pattern the credproxy path uses for
  Anthropic.
- Update `examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml` to wire
  `codexProviderSecretRef: openai-secrets`.

**Out of scope (other phases):**

- The Codex subagent package itself (`internal/subagent/codex/`) — phase-02.
- The Codex container image and entrypoint (`cmd/codex-subagent/`) — phase-03.
- The per-level vendor switch in `ResolveProvider` — phase-04.
- The heterogeneous integration example and end-to-end test — phase-06.

---

## Deliverables

| Deliverable | Verification |
|---|---|
| `images.codexSubagent` in values.yaml + `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` in deployment.yaml | `helm template` renders env var; `--set images.codexSubagent.tag=v1.2.3` passes through correctly |
| `ProviderDefaults.CodexImage` + vendor-aware `resolveImage` | Unit test: vendor=openai → CodexImage fallback; vendor=anthropic → Image fallback |
| `tideHelmProviderDefaults` populates CodexImage | `env_test.go` round-trip: `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE=ghcr.io/…/tide-codex-subagent:v1` → `ProviderDefaults.CodexImage=="ghcr.io/…/tide-codex-subagent:v1"` |
| `ProjectSpec.CodexProviderSecretRef` CRD field + deepcopy | `api/v1alpha1` schema test passes; `make generate` exits 0 |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` injected from `codexProviderSecretRef` when vendor=openai | Controller unit tests: Job spec for a Codex-vendor level contains `envFrom: [{secretRef: {name: openai-secrets}}]` |
| Dogfood example wires `codexProviderSecretRef: openai-secrets` | YAML parses against updated schema |
| No secret material inlined in any manifest or chart value | `grep -r "sk-" charts/` returns empty |
| `make verify-import-firewall` + `make verify-dispatch-imports` green | Zero Codex/OpenAI imports in `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` |
| `make test` exits 0 | All existing + new unit tests pass offline |

---

## Verification Gates

1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit tests plus the new vendor-image
   resolution and secret-injection tests pass with no network calls.
2. `make verify-import-firewall` and `make verify-dispatch-imports` green —
   zero `internal/subagent/codex` or OpenAI SDK imports visible in
   `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.
3. `helm template` with default values renders `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` in the
   manager Deployment; `helm template --set images.codexSubagent.tag=v9`
   renders the overridden tag.
4. `helm template` with `--set subagent.defaults.image=…` and
   `--set images.codexSubagent.repository=…` renders both image channels
   independently — confirming Claude and Codex image defaults are decoupled.
5. Unit test explicitly proves `resolveImage` returns `CodexImage` when
   `vendor="openai"` and no per-level/per-project Image override is set,
   and returns `Image` (Claude default) when `vendor="anthropic"`.
6. Unit test explicitly proves the Job pod spec contains
   `envFrom: [{secretRef: {name: "openai-secrets"}}]` when the level's
   vendor resolves to `"openai"` and `codexProviderSecretRef` is set.
7. No OpenAI API call runs in tests unless `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set —
   the suite passes fully offline.
8. No secret material is inlined in any manifest or chart value.

---

## Plan Decomposition

Three Plans — wave 1 has two independent plans that touch orthogonal surfaces
(Helm + Go-runtime vs. CRD API types); wave 2's single plan depends on both
and delivers the observable operator contract.

```
plan-05-01-codex-image-helm-defaults  ──┐
                                         ├──▶  plan-05-03-openai-secret-job-injection
plan-05-02-codex-provider-secret-ref  ──┘
```

**Wave assignment (illustrative — authoritative wave derived by Kahn over
the Task DAG):**

| Plan | Wave | Depends on |
|---|---|---|
| `plan-05-01-codex-image-helm-defaults` | 1 | — |
| `plan-05-02-codex-provider-secret-ref` | 1 | — |
| `plan-05-03-openai-secret-job-injection` | 2 | `plan-05-01-codex-image-helm-defaults`, `plan-05-02-codex-provider-secret-ref` |
PHASEBRIEF

| `make verify-import-firewall` + `make verify-dispatch-imports` green | Zero Codex/OpenAI imports in `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` |
| `make test` exits 0 | All existing + new unit tests pass offline |

---

## Verification Gates

1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit tests plus the new vendor-image
   resolution and secret-injection tests pass with no network calls.
2. `make verify-import-firewall` and `make verify-dispatch-imports` green —
   zero `internal/subagent/codex` or OpenAI SDK imports visible in
   `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.
3. `helm template` with default values renders `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` in the
   manager Deployment; `helm template --set images.codexSubagent.tag=v9`
   renders the overridden tag.
4. `helm template` with both image channel overrides renders Claude and Codex
   image defaults independently — confirming the two channels are decoupled.
5. Unit test: `resolveImage` returns `CodexImage` when vendor="openai" and no
   per-level/per-project Image override is set; returns `Image` when
   vendor="anthropic".
6. Unit test: Job pod spec contains envFrom secretRef when the level's
   vendor resolves to "openai" and `codexProviderSecretRef` is set.
7. No OpenAI API call runs in tests unless OPENAI_API_KEY is set —
   the suite passes fully offline.
8. No secret material is inlined in any manifest or chart value.

---

## Plan Decomposition

Three Plans — wave 1 has two independent plans that touch orthogonal surfaces
(Helm + Go-runtime vs. CRD API types); wave 2's single plan depends on both
and delivers the observable operator contract.

python3 -c "
content = '''# Phase Brief -- phase-05-chart-secret-wiring

**Phase:** phase-05-chart-secret-wiring
**Milestone:** milestone-03-hetero-integration
**Wave position:** Milestone wave 2 -- depends on phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch; runs in
parallel with no other currently-planned milestone phases.

---

## Objective

Wire the Codex image default, the Codex provider-Secret reference, and the
OPENAI_API_KEY env-injection into dispatch Job pods -- closing the gap between
phase-04s resolved Vendor:\"openai\" output and an actual runnable Codex Job
pod. Phase-04 delivers the per-level vendor switch in the controller; this
phase delivers the Helm chart surface that operators configure and the
controller-side secret injection that makes the vendor selection executable.

Three orthogonal concerns -- Helm defaults, the Project CRD API field, and
the Job-pod secret injection -- compose into a complete operator experience:
set subagent.levels.task.vendor: openai (or
project.spec.subagent.levels.task.vendor: openai) plus
project.spec.codexProviderSecretRef: my-openai-secret, and TIDE dispatches
Codex Jobs with OPENAI_API_KEY populated -- no credproxy required, no host
config mounted, no OAuth.

---

## Scope

**In scope:**

- Add images.codexSubagent image block to charts/tide/values.yaml --
  mirrors the existing claudeSubagent entry; provides the cluster-wide
  Codex image default (ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent).
- Add CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var to charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml
  -- the second image-default channel alongside CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE,
  consumed by the controllers tideHelmProviderDefaults.
- Extend ProviderDefaults with a CodexImage string field
  (internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go); make resolveImage
  vendor-aware -- when the resolved vendor is \"openai\" the fallback is
  helmDefaults.CodexImage instead of helmDefaults.Image.
- Populate ProviderDefaults.CodexImage from CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE in
  cmd/manager/env.go (the tideHelmProviderDefaults constructor).
- Add CodexProviderSecretRef string to ProjectSpec
  (api/v1alpha1/project_types.go) -- the per-Project Secret name carrying
  OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY; mirrors the existing
  providerSecretRef field. Regenerate zz_generated.deepcopy.go.
- Extend all four Job-creating controller reconcilers
  (task_controller.go, milestone_controller.go, phase_controller.go,
  plan_controller.go) to inject OPENAI_API_KEY from
  project.Spec.CodexProviderSecretRef when ResolveProvider returns
  Vendor:\"openai\" for the dispatched level -- following the same
  envFrom secretRef pattern the credproxy path uses for Anthropic.
- Update examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml to wire
  codexProviderSecretRef: openai-secrets.

**Out of scope (other phases):**

- The Codex subagent package itself (internal/subagent/codex/) -- phase-02.
- The Codex container image and entrypoint (cmd/codex-subagent/) -- phase-03.
- The per-level vendor switch in ResolveProvider -- phase-04.
- The heterogeneous integration example and end-to-end test -- phase-06.

---

## Deliverables

| Deliverable | Verification |
|---|---|
| images.codexSubagent in values.yaml + CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE in deployment.yaml | helm template renders env var; --set images.codexSubagent.tag=v1.2.3 passes through |
| ProviderDefaults.CodexImage + vendor-aware resolveImage | Unit test: vendor=openai => CodexImage fallback; vendor=anthropic => Image fallback |
| tideHelmProviderDefaults populates CodexImage | env_test.go round-trip: CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE=... sets ProviderDefaults.CodexImage |
| ProjectSpec.CodexProviderSecretRef CRD field + deepcopy | api/v1alpha1 schema test passes; make generate exits 0 |
| OPENAI_API_KEY injected from codexProviderSecretRef when vendor=openai | Controller unit tests: Job spec contains envFrom secretRef for Codex-vendor level |
| Dogfood example wires codexProviderSecretRef: openai-secrets | YAML parses against updated schema |
| No secret material inlined in any manifest or chart value | grep for raw key bytes in charts/ returns empty |
| make verify-import-firewall + make verify-dispatch-imports green | Zero Codex/OpenAI imports in controller or cmd/manager |
| make test exits 0 | All existing + new unit tests pass offline |

---

## Verification Gates

1. make test exits 0 -- all existing unit tests plus the new vendor-image
   resolution and secret-injection tests pass with no network calls.
2. make verify-import-firewall and make verify-dispatch-imports green --
   zero internal/subagent/codex or OpenAI SDK imports visible in
   internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.
3. helm template with default values renders CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE in the
   manager Deployment; helm template --set images.codexSubagent.tag=v9
   renders the overridden tag.
4. helm template renders Claude and Codex image defaults independently --
   confirming the two channels are decoupled.
5. Unit test: resolveImage returns CodexImage when vendor=\"openai\" and no
   per-level/per-project Image override is set; returns Image when
   vendor=\"anthropic\".
6. Unit test: Job pod spec contains envFrom secretRef when the levels
   vendor resolves to \"openai\" and codexProviderSecretRef is set.
7. No OpenAI API call runs in tests unless OPENAI_API_KEY is set --
   the suite passes fully offline.
8. No secret material is inlined in any manifest or chart value.

---

## Plan Decomposition

Three Plans -- wave 1 has two independent plans that touch orthogonal surfaces
(Helm + Go-runtime vs. CRD API types); wave 2s single plan depends on both
and delivers the observable operator contract.

Wave 1 (parallel):
  plan-05-01-codex-image-helm-defaults
  plan-05-02-codex-provider-secret-ref

Wave 2 (join barrier):
  plan-05-03-openai-secret-job-injection
    depends on: plan-05-01-codex-image-helm-defaults, plan-05-02-codex-provider-secret-ref
'''
with open('/workspace/envelopes/80621bfb-519b-469c-ad37-49281e0b0dad/children/PHASE.md', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
print('Written')
"

tool_input.description: Read secret injection logic in task controller

tool_input.command:
cat > /workspace/envelopes/80621bfb-519b-469c-ad37-49281e0b0dad/children/PHASE.md << 'PHASEBRIEF'
# Phase Brief — phase-05-chart-secret-wiring

**Phase:** `phase-05-chart-secret-wiring`
**Milestone:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
**Wave position:** Milestone wave 2 — depends on `phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch`; runs in
parallel with no other currently-planned milestone phases.

---

## Objective

Wire the Codex image default, the Codex provider-Secret reference, and the
`OPENAI_API_KEY` env-injection into dispatch Job pods — closing the gap between
phase-04's resolved `Vendor:"openai"` output and an actual runnable Codex Job
pod. Phase-04 delivers the per-level vendor switch in the controller; this
phase delivers the Helm chart surface that operators configure and the
controller-side secret injection that makes the vendor selection executable.

Three orthogonal concerns — Helm defaults, the Project CRD API field, and
the Job-pod secret injection — compose into a complete operator experience:
set `subagent.levels.task.vendor: openai` (or
`project.spec.subagent.levels.task.vendor: openai`) plus
`project.spec.codexProviderSecretRef: my-openai-secret`, and TIDE dispatches
Codex Jobs with `OPENAI_API_KEY` populated — no credproxy required, no host
config mounted, no OAuth.

---

## Scope

**In scope:**

- Add `images.codexSubagent` image block to `charts/tide/values.yaml` —
  mirrors the existing `claudeSubagent` entry; provides the cluster-wide
  Codex image default (`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`).
- Add `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` env var to `charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml`
  — the second image-default channel alongside `CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE`,
  consumed by the controller's `tideHelmProviderDefaults`.
- Extend `ProviderDefaults` with a `CodexImage string` field
  (`internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go`); make `resolveImage`
  vendor-aware — when the resolved vendor is `"openai"` the fallback is
  `helmDefaults.CodexImage` instead of `helmDefaults.Image`.
- Populate `ProviderDefaults.CodexImage` from `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` in
  `cmd/manager/env.go` (the `tideHelmProviderDefaults` constructor).
- Add `CodexProviderSecretRef string` to `ProjectSpec`
  (`api/v1alpha1/project_types.go`) — the per-Project Secret name carrying
  `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `CODEX_API_KEY`; mirrors the existing
  `providerSecretRef` field. Regenerate `zz_generated.deepcopy.go`.
- Extend all four Job-creating controller reconcilers
  (`task_controller.go`, `milestone_controller.go`, `phase_controller.go`,
  `plan_controller.go`) to inject `OPENAI_API_KEY` from
  `project.Spec.CodexProviderSecretRef` when `ResolveProvider` returns
  `Vendor:"openai"` for the dispatched level — following the same
  `envFrom: [{secretRef: {name: …}}]` pattern the credproxy path uses for
  Anthropic.
- Update `examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml` to wire
  `codexProviderSecretRef: openai-secrets`.

**Out of scope (other phases):**

- The Codex subagent package itself (`internal/subagent/codex/`) — phase-02.
- The Codex container image and entrypoint (`cmd/codex-subagent/`) — phase-03.
- The per-level vendor switch in `ResolveProvider` — phase-04.
- The heterogeneous integration example and end-to-end test — phase-06.

---

## Deliverables

| Deliverable | Verification |
|---|---|
| `images.codexSubagent` in values.yaml + `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` in deployment.yaml | `helm template` renders env var; `--set images.codexSubagent.tag=v1.2.3` passes through correctly |
| `ProviderDefaults.CodexImage` + vendor-aware `resolveImage` | Unit test: vendor=openai → CodexImage fallback; vendor=anthropic → Image fallback |
| `tideHelmProviderDefaults` populates CodexImage | `env_test.go` round-trip: `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE=ghcr.io/…/tide-codex-subagent:v1` → `ProviderDefaults.CodexImage=="ghcr.io/…/tide-codex-subagent:v1"` |
| `ProjectSpec.CodexProviderSecretRef` CRD field + deepcopy | `api/v1alpha1` schema test passes; `make generate` exits 0 |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` injected from `codexProviderSecretRef` when vendor=openai | Controller unit tests: Job spec for a Codex-vendor level contains `envFrom: [{secretRef: {name: openai-secrets}}]` |
| Dogfood example wires `codexProviderSecretRef: openai-secrets` | YAML parses against updated schema |
| No secret material inlined in any manifest or chart value | `grep -r "sk-" charts/` returns empty |
| `make verify-import-firewall` + `make verify-dispatch-imports` green | Zero Codex/OpenAI imports in `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` |
| `make test` exits 0 | All existing + new unit tests pass offline |

---

## Verification Gates

1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit tests plus the new vendor-image
   resolution and secret-injection tests pass with no network calls.
2. `make verify-import-firewall` and `make verify-dispatch-imports` green —
   zero `internal/subagent/codex` or OpenAI SDK imports visible in
   `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.
3. `helm template` with default values renders `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` in the
   manager Deployment; `helm template --set images.codexSubagent.tag=v9`
   renders the overridden tag.
4. `helm template` with `--set subagent.defaults.image=…` and
   `--set images.codexSubagent.repository=…` renders both image channels
   independently — confirming Claude and Codex image defaults are decoupled.
5. Unit test explicitly proves `resolveImage` returns `CodexImage` when
   `vendor="openai"` and no per-level/per-project Image override is set,
   and returns `Image` (Claude default) when `vendor="anthropic"`.
6. Unit test explicitly proves the Job pod spec contains
   `envFrom: [{secretRef: {name: "openai-secrets"}}]` when the level's
   vendor resolves to `"openai"` and `codexProviderSecretRef` is set.
7. No OpenAI API call runs in tests unless `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set —
   the suite passes fully offline.
8. No secret material is inlined in any manifest or chart value.

---

## Plan Decomposition

Three Plans — wave 1 has two independent plans that touch orthogonal surfaces
(Helm + Go-runtime vs. CRD API types); wave 2's single plan depends on both
and delivers the observable operator contract.

```
plan-05-01-codex-image-helm-defaults  ──┐
                                         ├──▶  plan-05-03-openai-secret-job-injection
plan-05-02-codex-provider-secret-ref  ──┘
```

**Wave assignment (illustrative — authoritative wave derived by Kahn over
the Task DAG):**

| Plan | Wave | Depends on |
|---|---|---|
| `plan-05-01-codex-image-helm-defaults` | 1 | — |
| `plan-05-02-codex-provider-secret-ref` | 1 | — |
| `plan-05-03-openai-secret-job-injection` | 2 | `plan-05-01-codex-image-helm-defaults`, `plan-05-02-codex-provider-secret-ref` |
PHASEBRIEF
